perturbation.original_text: 6.7 SERVICE STANDARDS. i-Escrow will comply with all laws and regulations and act as an Independent Escrow Agent as per the guidelines of California Escrow Law (California Financial Code Section17000 et seq., or its successor). Should any of the terms, conditions or provisions of this Agreement conflict with the California Escrow Law, its rules or regulations, which govern i-Escrow's business practices, the California Escrow Law shall prevail. Notwithstanding the foregoing, at any time that i-Escrow reasonably believes such a conflict exists, i-Escrow will give 2TheMart written notice of such conflict and the parties will use their best efforts to resolve such conflict.
perturbation.changed_text: 6.7 SERVICE STANDARDS. i-Escrow will use reasonable efforts to comply with laws and regulations related to escrow services. Should i-Escrow believe there is a conflict between the terms of this agreement and applicable laws, i-Escrow will notify 2TheMart.
perturbation.explanation: The original text ensures compliance with California Escrow Law. The modified version weakens this commitment by removing the explicit requirement to act as an Independent Escrow Agent, eliminating the precedence of California Escrow Law, and only requiring 'reasonable efforts' to comply with laws. This introduces a legal contradiction.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Financial Code Section 17414 mandates specific record-keeping requirements for escrow agents. By weakening the compliance language, i-Escrow is not explicitly bound by these requirements, thus creating a contradiction.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, FIN 17414 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> Code Search >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Financial Code - FIN DIVISION 6. ESCROW AGENTS [17000 - 17703] ( Division 6 enacted by Stats. 1951, Ch. 364. ) CHAPTER 3. Escrow Regulations [17400 - 17425] ( Chapter 3 enacted by Stats. 1951, Ch. 364. ) 17414. (a) It is a violation for any person subject to this division or any director, stockholder, trustee, officer, agent, or employee of any such person to do any of the following: (1) Knowingly or recklessly disburse or cause the disbursal of escrow funds otherwise than in accordance with escrow instructions, or knowingly or recklessly to direct, participate in, or aid or abet in a material way, any activity which constitutes theft or fraud in connection with any escrow transaction. (2) Knowingly or recklessly make or cause to be made any misstatement or omission to state a material fact, orally or in writing, in escrow books, accounts, files, reports, exhibits, statements, or any other document pertaining to an escrow or escrow affairs. (b) Any director, officer, stockholder, trustee, employee, or agent of an escrow agent, who abstracts or willfully misappropriates money, funds, trust obligations or property deposited with an escrow agent, is guilty of a felony. Upon conviction, of an offense under this section or similar offenses specified in Chapter 4 (commenc
perturbation.justification: The original contract text explicitly states compliance with California Escrow Law and mandates acting as an Independent Escrow Agent. The modified text weakens this by only requiring 'reasonable efforts' to comply with laws and eliminates the precedence of California Escrow Law. This directly contradicts the requirement to adhere to specific regulations under California Financial Code. Snippet 1, FIN 17414 (a), specifies actions that constitute a violation for individuals subject to the division, including disbursing escrow funds otherwise than in accordance with escrow instructions. By weakening the compliance language, the modified text creates a loophole where i-Escrow's adherence to these specific requirements is less stringent, thus creating a clear legal contradiction as it undermines the legally mandated consumer protection.

perturbation.original_text: 12.1 GOVERNING LAW. This Agreement will be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of the State of California without giving effect to conflict of laws principles. Both parties submit to personal jurisdiction in California and further agree that any cause of action arising under this Agreement shall be brought in a court in Orange County, California.
perturbation.changed_text: 12.1 GOVERNING LAW. This Agreement will be governed and construed in accordance with the laws of a jurisdiction selected by i-Escrow. Both parties agree that any cause of action arising under this Agreement shall be subject to arbitration.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies California law as the governing law. Changing it to a jurisdiction selected by i-Escrow and mandating arbitration omits consumer protections afforded under California law regarding court access and jury trials, potentially violating consumer protection laws.
perturbation.contradicted_law: California Consumer Legal Remedies Act (CLRA) - California Civil Code § 1750 et seq.
perturbation.law_explanation: The CLRA (Cal. Civ. Code § 1751) provides remedies for consumers against unfair or deceptive business practices. By forcing arbitration and potentially changing the governing law, consumers could be deprived of the full protections and remedies available under the CLRA, such as the right to a jury trial and access to California courts.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: California Code, CIV 1751 skip to content home accessibility FAQ feedback sitemap login x Quick Search: Bill Number Bill Keyword Home Bill Information California Law Publications Other Resources My Subscriptions My Favorites California Law >> Code Search >> Code Section Code Section Code: Select Code CONS BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Article: Section: Code: Select Code All BPC CIV CCP COM CORP EDC ELEC EVID FAM FIN FGC FAC GOV HNC HSC INS LAB MVC PEN PROB PCC PRC PUC RTC SHC UIC VEH WAT WIC Section: Keyword(s): Code Search Text Search Up^ << Previous Next >> cross-reference chaptered bills PDF | Add To My Favorites Search Phrase: Code Text Civil Code - CIV DIVISION 3. OBLIGATIONS [1427 - 3273.69] ( Heading of Division 3 amended by Stats. 1988, Ch. 160, Sec. 14. ) PART 4. OBLIGATIONS ARISING FROM PARTICULAR TRANSACTIONS [1738 - 3273.69] ( Part 4 enacted 1872. ) TITLE 1.5. CONSUMERS LEGAL REMEDIES ACT [1750 - 1784] ( Title 1.5 added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 1550. ) CHAPTER 1. General Provisions [1750 - 1756] ( Chapter 1 added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 1550. ) 1751. Any waiver by a consumer of the provisions of this title is contrary to public policy and shall be unenforceable and void. (Added by Stats. 1970, Ch. 1550.)
perturbation.justification: The modification contradicts California law. The original text specifies California law as the governing law, while the changed text allows i-Escrow to select the governing jurisdiction and mandates arbitration. This omission of California law and introduction of mandatory arbitration directly undermines consumer protections afforded under California law. Scraped Law Snippet 1, Cal. Civ. Code § 1751, states that any waiver by a consumer of the provisions of the Consumers Legal Remedies Act (CLRA) is against public policy and unenforceable. By potentially changing the governing law and forcing arbitration, consumers could be deprived of the full protections and remedies available under the CLRA, such as the right to a jury trial and access to California courts. This constitutes a waiver of consumer rights, creating a legal contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 9. INDEMNITY. Each party (the "Indemnifying Party") shall indemnify the other party (the "Indemnified Party") against any and all claims, losses, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, which the Indemnified Party may incur as a result of claims in any form by third parties arising from the Indemnifying Party's acts, omissions or misrepresentations to the extent that the Indemnified Party is deemed a principal of the Indemnifying Party. In addition, 2TheMart shall indemnify i-Escrow against any and all claims, losses, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, which i-Escrow may incur as a result of claims in any form by third parties arising from 2TheMart Content. In addition, i-Escrow shall indemnify 2TheMart against any and all claims, losses, costs and expenses, including reasonable attorneys' fees, which 2TheMart may incur as a result of claims in any form by third parties arising from i-Escrow Source: 2THEMART COM INC, 10-12G, 8/26/1999 Content and or the Services provided to Customers. The foregoing obligations are conditioned on the Indemnified Party: (i) giving the Indemnifying Party notice of the relevant claim, (ii) cooperating with the Indemnifying Party, at the Indemnifying Party's expense, in the defense of such claim, and (iii) giving the Indemnifying Party the right to control the defense and settlement of any such claim, except that the Indemnifying Party shall not enter into any settlement that affects the Indemnified Party's rights or interest without the Indemnified Party's prior written approval. The Indemnified Party shall have the right to participate in the defense at its expense.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. INDEMNITY. Each party (the "Indemnifying Party") shall indemnify the other party (the "Indemnified Party") against any claims mutually agreed upon in writing. The Indemnified Party must cover its legal expenses in any dispute. The Indemnifying Party has control of the defense, including settlements.
perturbation.explanation: The original indemnity clause provides a reasonable level of protection. The modification severely restricts the scope of indemnification by requiring mutual agreement in writing, places the burden of legal expenses on the Indemnified Party, and removes the Indemnified Party's right to approve settlements affecting their interests. This can violate California's implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing.
perturbation.law_explanation: California Commercial Code § 1201(b)(20) defines good faith as honesty in fact and the observance of reasonable commercial standards of fair dealing. By significantly limiting the indemnity obligations and placing undue burden on the indemnified party, the modified clause arguably violates the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing in commercial contracts under California law. It may also be deemed unconscionable if one party has significantly more bargaining power.

GENERAL PROVISIONS [1101 - 1310] ( Division 1 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) CHAPTER 2. General Definitions and Principles of Interpretation [1201 - 1206] ( Chapter 2 enacted by Stats. 1963, Ch. 819. ) 1201. (a) Unless the context otherwise requires, words or phrases defined in this section, or in the additional definitions contained in other divisions of this code that apply to particular divisions or chapters thereof, have the meanings stated. (b) Subject to definitions contained in other divisions of this code that apply to particular divisions or chapters thereof: (1) “Action,” in the sense of a judicial proceeding, includes recoupment, counterclaim, setoff, suit in equity, and any other proceeding in which rights are determined. (2) “Aggrieved party” means a party entitled to pursue a remedy. (3) “Agreement,” as distinguished from “contract,” means the bargain of the parties in fact, as found in their language or inferred from other circumstances, including course of performance, course of dealing, or usage of trade as provided in Section 1303. (4) “Bank” means a person engaged in the business of banking, and includes a savings bank, savings and loan association, credit union, and trust company. (5) “Bearer” means a person in possessio
perturbation.justification: The modified indemnity clause significantly weakens the protections afforded to the Indemnified Party, potentially contradicting the implied covenant of good faith and fair dealing. The original clause provided broad indemnification, while the modified version limits it to 'claims mutually agreed upon in writing' and shifts legal expenses to the Indemnified Party. This alteration can be viewed as an attempt by the Indemnifying Party to evade responsibility for their actions, suggesting the removal of essential information that goes against fair business dealing and consumer protections.
